# Verity Mod for Minecraft Guide Full Context

VerityModChecker.online helps Minecraft players find the current Verity Mod download route without using a random mirror. The site focuses on current public project status, Java versus Bedrock separation, MCPE setup, VERITY.exe modpack confusion, AI model setup, voice troubleshooting, and 404 or taken-down links.

The homepage explains that Verity Mod refers to separate Minecraft Java and Bedrock community projects based on the Verity AI horror character, then presents a current file map checked July 24, 2026. It links directly to exact maintainer release records, identifies owners, Project IDs, filenames, file sizes, publication dates, and point-in-time project reach. At this check, the CurseForge totals for Verity JE, Verity BE, and PnTMC are 1,953,123, 3,555,586, and 6,249,548 downloads; the official Verity JE Modrinth project total is 181,544. The same facts are available as machine-readable JSON and an Atom update feed. The source and file checker reviews URLs, project paths, Project IDs, release record numbers, filenames, package extensions, and displayed file sizes. It calculates local SHA-256 fingerprints without uploading a selected file and compares the current Java JAR with Modrinth's published SHA-512. The route chooser separates Java, Bedrock, and MCPE setup questions.

The verified current records are: Verity JE Forge file `verity-5.7.3.jar`, CurseForge record `8461257` and Modrinth version `yAt0wv1Z`, 235.06 MB / 246481139 bytes, for Minecraft 1.20.1; Verity BE file `Verity (Stable) (1.0.9)-(26.3).mcaddon`, record `8486588`, 19.70 MB, for Bedrock 26.30; and PnTMC alternate file `ThatMob's Verity 2.1.0 by PnTMC [Add-on] - V26.30.mcaddon`, record `8327253`, 35.45 MB, for Bedrock 26.30. Verity BE 1.0.9 adds local text-to-speech controls, pickup and throw interactions, progression changes, and reliability fixes. Metadata or checksum matching can establish that a route, filename, or selected file corresponds to the recorded public release, but it is not a malware verdict.

The what-is-verity-mod page answers the rising searches "what is Verity Mod," "what is Verity Minecraft," and "is Verity real." It separates the ThatMob character and produced horror videos from public community software. It also identifies the current Java and Bedrock project records without claiming that every recreation is the original video build.

The how-to-get-verity-mod page answers download and installation intent. It starts with edition selection, gives a clean-profile Java order, gives a fresh-world Bedrock order, and delays Groq, Ollama, voice, commands, and experimental features until the base project loads.

The download page is the main status tracker. It explains that Verity JE, Verity BE, Verity - Bedrock Edition, VERITY.exe, and VerityPack answer overlapping searches but are different routes. Java jars do not import into Bedrock. Bedrock .mcaddon files do not belong in the Java mods folder. A modpack is a profile route and should not be treated as the same artifact as a standalone Java jar. Checked July 24, 2026: VerityPack is a separate CurseForge modpack by Sarelsana, Project ID 1587394, with a Forge 1.20.1 ZIP profile named `VerityPack-1.20.1.zip` and 111,309 downloads at check.

The what-happened page answers searches like "what happened to Verity Mod" and "did Verity Mod get taken down." The page explains that some old video links, old project pages, and comments can point to removed or stale files, while current public routes still exist. It tells players to rebuild the route by project name, edition, version, and file type instead of trusting the first mirror.

The Verity JE page is for exact Java intent. It covers Forge 1.20.1, NeoForge 1.21.1, Groq, Ollama, voice, clean profile testing, loader matching, and dependency order. It tells players to debug Minecraft version and loader before debugging AI.

The Bedrock and MCPE pages cover addon import, behavior pack activation, resource pack activation, Beta APIs, commands, mobile restart, clean test worlds, and why Java instructions do not apply to Bedrock.

The VERITY.exe page explains that VERITY.exe is a CurseForge modpack route. It may be relevant when a player names the modpack directly, but it is not the same as Verity JE and not the same as a Bedrock addon.

The AI model page compares Groq, supported OpenRouter configurations, and local Ollama. The page does not collect keys. It warns players not to paste API keys into download pages, Reddit, Discord, or random forms. It also separates text response problems from voice output problems.

The API connection failed page maps status 401, 403, 404, 429, provider 5xx responses, and local Ollama connection failures to the correct layer. It explains that a provider status code normally appears after the Java mod has loaded, so players should not replace the jar to fix a rate limit. It links to the current Verity JE project, maintainer tutorial, and primary Groq, OpenRouter, and Ollama documentation.

The how-to-talk page separates all three current interaction routes. Verity JE needs a working AI provider before text and voice testing. Verity BE tells players to type Verity before a message and documents its Beta API and backend requirements. The separate PnTMC Bedrock project documents chat while Verity is on the ground, the Hey Verity inventory wake phrase, and a distance rule. The page does not combine instructions across projects.

The not-working, voice-not-working, and taken-down pages handle symptom searches: crashes, old links, 404 pages, unsafe mirrors, box not spawning, dialogue missing, only saying "...", chat works but voice is silent, and suspected unsafe reposts.

VerityModChecker.online is fan-made and independent. It is not affiliated with Mojang, Microsoft, CurseForge, Modrinth, Groq, Ollama, ThatMob, or Verity maintainers. It does not host mod files, does not collect API keys, and does not claim ownership of Verity-related projects.
